body:
## Summary
- Add `test/ProjectDistributed.toml` — a lean test project without heavy dependencies (XESMF, Reactant, CairoMakie, Enzyme, NCDatasets, Metal, oneAPI, AMDGPU) and with `CUDA_Runtime_jll` as a proper dependency
- Pipeline swaps it in with `mv` before running tests for faster resolution and precompilation
- Detect local CUDA toolkit version from `nvidia-smi` and write `test/LocalPreferences.toml` directly from bash — no extra Julia startup needed
- Remove `CUDA_Runtime_jll` from generic `test/Project.toml`
- Fix `inject_halo_communication_boundary_conditions` call signature in distributed Poisson solver tests (added location tuple argument)

## Motivation
After #5466 removed `CUDA_Runtime_jll` from test deps, the distributed GPU tests could no longer find the correct CUDA runtime. This PR properly configures the local CUDA toolkit via `LocalPreferences.toml` and also slims down the distributed test environment to avoid precompiling packages that aren't needed for distributed testing.

## Test plan
- [x] Distributed CPU tests pass
- [ ] <s>Distributed GPU tests pass <s/>

labels.description: Tests get priority in case of emergency evacuation
labels.name: testing 🧪

labels.description: Our plan for total cluster domination
labels.name: distributed 🕸️

title: Fix distributed tests: lean project, local CUDA detection, halo BC fix

body:
Oceananigans moves quite fast compared to other repos, so having only a 50 commit window in the benchmarks is quite restrictive. This data is simple text so it will not blow up the OceananigansBenchmarks repo too much. 

This PR increases the history from 50 commits to 5000 commits
title: Increment benchmarking commit history

body:
Remove `Adapt.adapt_structure` for `CommunicationBuffers` (unused since individual buffer types all adapt to `nothing`) and fix the south/north argument order in `on_architecture` to match the struct field order (west, east, south, north, ...), as per https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/5422#issuecomment-4116785493.

Closes #5422
title: Fix north/south buffer swap in CommunicationBuffers

body:
The type parameters in `IsopycnalSkewSymmetricDiffusivity` were scrambled after passing the `with_tracers` method. Leading to `required_halo_size` returning the type of the diffusivity rather than the actual required halo.

This PR fixes the bug

Found by @lgloege and @tlangfor when running distributed NumericalEarth simulations
title: (0.107.5) Bugfix `with_tracers` for `IsopycnalSkewSymmetricDiffusivity`
